column2: Description

Define types/structures for all CAST entities: Run, Baseline, Timeline, LKG, Contract check result, Diff, Finding. Required fields for lifecycle, comparability, and evidence linkage must be present. Invariants encoded as validation utilities or guard functions. Must match case_v0.md.

Implement validation for: timeline ownership checks, baseline immutability checks, LKG-in-same-timeline checks, finding-to-diff evidence checks. Violations must produce deterministic structured errors. Invariant checks callable from workflow stages. Depends on D-001.

column1: D-003: Implement contract check result schema and reason taxonomy
column2: Define pass/warn/fail status model with structured reason format. Blocking vs non-blocking flags. Environment mismatch = warn (fuzzy comparable). Schema/feature/data scope mismatch = fail. Check results must be persistable and consumable by analysis stage. Depends on D-001.

column1: D-004: Define contract/profile model and checker interface
column2: Canonical contract/profile shape and checker execution interface. Separate contract identity/version from run-level verdicts. Depends on D-001.

Implement lifecycle: created → prepared → checked → analyzed → closed, with failed as terminal state reachable from any stage. Illegal transitions blocked. Transition history auditable. Must align with workflow_v0.md. Depends on D-001.

column1: W-001a: Define synchronous SDK/CLI result contract
column2: Canonical result fields for lifecycle status, comparability, references, summaries, and errors. CLI renders from the same result contract returned by the SDK. Depends on D-001 and D-003.

Resolve timeline, baseline, previous run, active LKG, custom reference, contract, and source training run. Validate required metrics/artifacts on source run. Actionable error messages on failure. Depends on W-001, D-001, and R-003.

column1: W-002a: Implement first-run baseline bootstrap rule
column2: If no timeline exists, require explicit baseline_source_run_id. Initialize sentinel/timeline config from that baseline reference and reject baseline changes once the timeline exists. Depends on W-002 and P-001.
Execute contract checker, record pass/warn/fail. Persist check reasons, and do not start analysis before check completion. Depends on W-002, D-003, and D-004.

Comparable path: compute diffs against all references + generate findings. Non-comparable path: skip metric deltas, emit compatibility records only. Both paths produce summary outputs. Lifecycle transitions to analyzed deterministically. Depends on W-003, E-001, E-002.

column1: W-005: Implement optional LKG promotion stage
column2: Policy evaluation hook. Promote/hold decision record. LKG pointer update via gateway. Promotion only for closed runs. Promotion outcome auditable. Aligns with MLflow promote concept. Depends on W-004 and P-003.

Define default recipe that ships with MLflow-Monitor. Requires no user configuration. Reads whatever metrics/params/tags MLflow already has. No required_metrics, no required_artifacts, no contract gates. Default finding policy and standard summary output. System usable without any user-authored recipe. User-specified recipe overrides default cleanly. Depends on R-001.

column1: R-000a: Define zero-config default behavior details
column2: Define default source-run selection semantics for non-bootstrap runs, default contract/check behavior when optional evidence is absent, and explicit separation between recipe defaults and first-run baseline bootstrap. Depends on R-000 and D-004.

Define schema sections: identity/version, input binding, contract binding, metrics/slices binding, finding policy binding, output binding. Unknown/disallowed sections rejected. Must match recipe_v0.md. Depends on D-001.
Structural, referential, and constraint validation. Fails fast with specific, actionable error messages. Depends on R-001 and D-004.
Normalize recipe into executable run plan and resolve defaults. Deterministic for the same recipe/version. Depends on R-002.
Version identity and selection rules. Backward-safe behavior for old runs. Run always stores exact recipe version. Historical reproducibility preserved. Depends on R-003.

Implement comparison modes: baseline, previous, LKG, and structural compatibility record mode. Diff outputs are objective and reference-tagged. Missing previous/LKG handled as unavailable, not failure. Non-comparable run skips metric diff mode. Depends on D-001 and W-003.
Diff-to-finding mapping. Severity assignment. Evidence linkage. Every finding references evidence. Summary top findings generated. Depends on E-001 and D-001.
Minimal categories. Default severity rules. Default recommendation templates. Reasonable defaults for early pilots. Policy behavior configurable via recipe. Depends on E-002 and R-003.

Central write/read API. Enforce configurable namespace prefix for all writes and read-only access to training experiments. Implement sentinel run creation from explicit first-run baseline, sequence index assignment, idempotency key check, LKG resolution query, and read visibility rule. Depends on W-001 and D-001.
Implement writes for prepare, check, analyze, and close stages. Partial failure handling explicit. Depends on P-001, P-002a, and W-004.

column1: P-002a: Define canonical persisted artifact schemas
column2: Canonical schema contracts for diffs, findings, summaries, and persisted metadata, with stable versioning rules for stored monitoring artifacts. Depends on D-001, E-001, E-002, and P-001.

column1: P-003: LKG metadata and retrieval semantics
column2: Promotion persistence, atomic active/superseded tag update, and deterministic LKG retrieval. Depends on P-002.
Retrieve ordered runs for timeline by sequence_index. Include lifecycle and comparability statuses. Deterministic ordering. Non-comparable runs visible. Depends on P-002.

Resolve anchor run. Return runs and outputs from anchor onward. Aggregate summary from stored outputs. No recomputation required. Non-comparable runs explicitly listed. Depends on Q-001, E-001, E-002.

column1: Q-003: LKG and baseline preset window queries
column2: Baseline-anchored preset. LKG-anchored preset. Presets resolve correctly and consistently. Outputs match anchor-window semantics. Depends on Q-002 and P-003.

column1: T-001: Build acceptance scenario test harness
column2: Implement acceptance scenarios A-J from acceptance_v0.md. Each scenario has deterministic pass/fail assertions. Failures provide actionable diagnostics. Depends on all P0 tasks in D, W, R, E, P, Q workstreams.
Automated checks for all critical invariants from case_v0.md. Any invariant breach fails CI. Invariant reports identify breach stage/entity. Depends on T-001 and D-002.
Basic concurrency and timeline-size stress tests. Persistence and query behavior under load. No semantic corruption under stress. Known performance baselines documented. Depends on T-001 and Q-002.

column1: O-001: Keep design docs synchronized with implementation
column2: Update docs for any accepted behavior deltas during implementation. No major behavior exists only in code and not in docs. Continuous task.
Lifecycle-state troubleshooting. Comparability-fail diagnostics. Persistence-fail diagnostics. Operator can diagnose top failure classes without reading source code. Depends on W-004, P-002, T-001.
Recipe setup. Baseline pinning. Contract setup. Expected outputs and interpretation guide. New pilot team can execute first run with minimal hand-holding. Depends on R-003 and W-004.
